entities.name: WEATHER AND CLIMATE INFORMATION SERVICES (WCIS)
entities.description: Services providing weather and climate data to farmers to inform decisions

entities.name: MULTI-DISCIPLINARY WORKING GROUP (MWG)
entities.description: A co-production initiative in Senegal designed to influence farmers' uptake of weather and climate information

entities.name: SENEGAL
entities.description: The country where the study on WCIS and MWG was conducted

entities.name: WEATHER AND CLIMATE INFORMATION (WCI)
entities.description: Data and knowledge regarding weather and climate used by farmers

entities.name: CLIMATE VARIABILITY
entities.description: Fluctuations in climate patterns that farmers need to adapt to

entities.name: SURVEY METHODS
entities.description: Innovative tools used to collect data for the assessment of WCIS effectiveness

entities.name: ANALYTICAL APPROACHES
entities.description: Rigorous methods used to control for self-selection bias and establish causal linkages

entities.name: SELF-SELECTION BIAS
entities.description: A potential bias in data where participants select themselves into a group

entities.name: FARM MANAGEMENT RESPONSES
entities.description: Actions taken by farmers in response to climate information

entities.name: SEASONAL FORECASTS
entities.description: Long-term weather predictions used to inform farming decisions

entities.name: IMPROVED SEED
entities.description: High-quality seeds used by farmers in response to seasonal forecasts

entities.name: FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Soil nutrients applied by farmers in response to seasonal forecasts

entities.name: MANURE
entities.description: Organic fertilizer applied by farmers in response to seasonal forecasts

entities.name: CROP DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The practice of growing multiple types of crops

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY APPROACHES
entities.description: Collaborative methods used in providing tailored climate information and advisory services

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns
